book_title: The Body Keeps the Score: Brain, Mind, and Body in the Healing of Trauma
out_of_band_chapters_note: ch2/ch3/ch16 sit at 0.75-0.79 vs the strict expository 0.80 floor; all pass the general 70-130% per-chapter law. Accepted as marginal; no end-load bias.

Atmospheric voice intro (Darwin published The Expression of the Emotions in 1872, contrasted with Origin and Descent). Carries no mechanism or study; the chapter's argument begins cleanly at ch5§2¶6 where Darwin's biological theory of emotion is stated. Removed to tighten over-retained chapter.

Authorial voice reflection ('The first time I encountered this passage, I reread it with growing excitement...'). A personal-resonance passage that restates the gut/heart visceral-emotion point already established by the Darwin pneumogastric-nerve mechanism in ch5§2¶11. No new study, data, or named mechanism.

Restated framing that 'almost all mental suffering involves relationships or arousal regulation.' Conceptually redundant with the social-safety and neuroception material retained in ch5§2¶26 and ch5§2¶32. No study finding or named mechanism.

Voice passage reinforcing 'social support is not the same as being in the presence of others / reciprocity.' Restates the social-support point already carried by the study-bearing unit ch5§2¶26 (disaster-response studies; Kagan). Cut as redundancy.

Restatement of the voice-tone/safety-signal mechanism ('yelling leads to dysregulation; dogs respond to tone'). Redundant with the mirror-neuron/facial-attunement mechanism (ch5§2¶22) and the polyvagal synthesis on vocal/facial communication (ch5§2¶65). No unique mechanism or data.
